title: Ephesians 1:3
message: Praise be to the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us in the heavenly realms with every spiritual blessing in Christ.
notes: Every spiritual blessing — not some, not most. The full inventory of heaven's resources has already been granted to me in Christ. I am not waiting to be blessed; I am learning to access what I already have.

title: Ephesians 1:7
message: In him we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins, in accordance with the riches of his grace.
notes: Redemption and forgiveness are possessions, not petitions. In Him I have them — present tense, already secured. The riches of His grace are the measure of what I carry.

title: Ephesians 1:11
message: In him we were also chosen, having been predestined according to the plan of him who works out everything in conformity with the purpose of his will.
notes: Chosen in Him before time — my identity was settled in eternity, not in the circumstances of my birth or history. I walk as someone who was selected, not someone searching for belonging.

title: Colossians 2:10
message: And in Christ you have been brought to fullness. He is the head over every power and authority.
notes: Brought to fullness — there is no lack in my spiritual position. I am complete in the One who is above every power and authority. Nothing ranks above what I have in Christ.

title: 2 Corinthians 5:21
message: God made him who had no sin to be sin for us, so that in him we might become the righteousness of God.
notes: I do not have righteousness — I am the righteousness of God in Christ. It is not a quality I develop; it is an identity I inhabit. In Him, I stand before God with nothing lacking.

title: Colossians 1:14
message: In whom we have redemption, the forgiveness of sins.
notes: Short and absolute — in Christ, redemption and forgiveness are mine. No process, no probation, no re-qualifying. These are settled realities I live from, not reach toward.

title: Romans 8:1
message: Therefore, there is now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.
notes: The verdict over my life changed the moment I came into Christ. No condemnation — not reduced, not conditional, not temporary. This is my permanent legal standing before God.

title: 1 Corinthians 1:30
message: It is because of him that you are in Christ Jesus, who has become for us wisdom from God — that is, our righteousness, holiness and redemption.
notes: Christ has become my wisdom, righteousness, holiness, and redemption. Every dimension of what I need is a Person I am already in. I do not pursue these things apart from Him — I find them all in Him.

title: Ephesians 2:6
message: And God raised us up with Christ and seated us with him in the heavenly realms in Christ Jesus.
notes: Seated — not striving, not standing, not waiting. My position in Christ is a place of rest and authority. I currently occupy a seat in the heavenly realms, far above every principality and power.

title: Ephesians 2:10
message: For we are God's handiwork, created in Christ Jesus to do good works, which God prepared in advance for us to do.
notes: Created in Christ Jesus — my purpose was embedded at the point of new creation. The works God prepared for me already exist; I am walking into something pre-built, not improvising my way through life.

title: Philippians 4:13
message: I can do all this through him who gives me strength.
notes: Capacity in Christ is not self-confidence — it is a relational strength drawn from union with Him. Whatever I face, I face it as someone in whom the strength of Christ operates.

title: Colossians 2:3
message: In whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
notes: All the treasures of wisdom and knowledge are in Christ — and I am in Christ. The storehouse is not outside me; my union with Him gives me access to everything hidden in Him.

title: 2 Corinthians 1:20
message: For no matter how many promises God has made, they are 'Yes' in Christ. And so through him the 'Amen' is spoken by us to the glory of God.
notes: Every promise in scripture has already been answered 'Yes' in Christ. I do not negotiate for promises — I enforce what has already been confirmed. My 'Amen' is an agreement with what God has already said.

title: Ephesians 1:4
message: For he chose us in him before the creation of the world to be holy and blameless in his sight.
notes: Chosen before creation — my selection predates the universe. God did not choose me based on what He saw me become; He chose me before anything existed. My standing is rooted in eternity.

title: Galatians 3:26-27
message: So in Christ Jesus you are all children of God through faith, for all of you who were baptized into Christ have clothed yourselves with Christ.
notes: Clothed with Christ — He is what people encounter when they encounter me. My identity is wrapped in His. I do not represent myself; I carry and display the One I am clothed in.

title: Romans 6:11
message: In the same way, count yourselves dead to sin but alive to God in Christ Jesus.
notes: Count is a deliberate act of reckoning — I decide what is true about me based on my position in Christ, not based on what I feel. Dead to sin, alive to God: this is the accounting I live by.

title: Colossians 3:3
message: For you died, and your life is now hidden with Christ in God.
notes: Hidden with Christ in God — doubly concealed. My true life is not visible to the natural realm. What the enemy sees is not where I actually live. I am kept in the safest place in the universe.

title: 1 Corinthians 15:22
message: For as in Adam all die, so in Christ all will be made alive.
notes: Two unions, two outcomes. In Adam — death. In Christ — life. My union with Christ has overridden everything I inherited in Adam. I live now under a new federal head and a new outcome.

title: Ephesians 3:6
message: This mystery is that through the gospel the Gentiles are heirs together with Israel, members together of one body, and sharers together in the promise in Christ Jesus.
notes: Sharers in the promise — in Christ, no inheritance is withheld from me based on background or origin. The mystery of the gospel is that I am a full heir, not a partial one.

title: Philippians 3:9
message: And be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own that comes from the law, but that which is through faith in Christ — the righteousness that comes from God on the basis of faith.
notes: Paul's singular ambition was to be found in Him. Not to achieve righteousness, but to be discovered already inside the One who is righteousness. That is my posture — not striving, but abiding.
